name: Dubbo Infection Outcomes Study (DIOS)
denominator_method: Consecutive patients presenting to primary care with acute EBV, Ross River virus, or Q fever confirmed by serology, enrolled at acute infection.
time_zero_definition: Onset of acute, serologically confirmed infection
funders: National Health and Medical Research Council (Australia)
notes: Landmark prospective inception cohort showing a stereotyped post-infective fatigue syndrome across three unrelated pathogens, with risk predicted by acute-illness severity rather than the specific microbe. Compared across pathogens; no unexposed control group.

publications.title: Post-infective and chronic fatigue syndromes precipitated by viral and non-viral pathogens: prospective cohort study
publications.authors: Hickie I, Davenport T, Wakefield D, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: met diagnostic criteria for a chronic fatigue syndrome at 6 months
findings.source_locator: Results (approx. 11% met CFS criteria at 6 months)
